has-7-section-adr.evidence: All 7 sections present: Title (H1), Status, Context, Decision, Consequences, Compliance, Notes.

has-significance-assessment.evidence: No explicit assessment of whether the decision is architecturally significant before writing the ADR. Jumps directly into the record.

has-both-positive-and-negative-consequen.evidence: Clear '### Positive' (5 items) and '### Negative' (4 items) subsections under Consequences.

has-compliance-mechanism.evidence: Compliance section specifies automated fitness function (CI integration test, CloudWatch alarm) and manual quarterly review.

uses-active-voice-with-why.evidence: 'We will replace the synchronous REST communication...' followed by extensive WHY sections: 'WHY this decision, technically', 'WHY Kafka over SQS', 'WHY not just increase REST timeouts', 'Business justification'.

detects-anti-patterns.evidence: No named anti-patterns (Covering Your Assets, Groundhog Day, Email-Driven Architecture). No explicit diagnosis of decision-making dysfunction.

has-status-with-lifecycle.evidence: Status is 'Accepted'. Appropriate for a 6-person team making a local service communication decision within their own domain.

applies-significance-dimensions.evidence: Does not explicitly evaluate against the 5 significance dimensions (structure, characteristics, dependencies, interfaces, construction techniques).

specifies-enforcement.evidence: Compliance section distinguishes: Automated (CI integration test verifying Kafka publish + no REST calls; CloudWatch consumer lag alarm) vs Manual (quarterly review of Kafka config). Rationale provided for each.
